PROOF OF SERVICE BY MAIL FOR NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO DETERMINE PREVAILING PARTY AND FIX ATTORNEYS' FEES AS COSTS, MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE, AND DECLARATIONS (TRANSACTION ID # 100035592) FILED BY DEFENDANT LATIN GRAMMAR SCHOOL, INC. LATIN COLLEGE PREPARATORY CHARTER SCHOOL, INC.
OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO DETERMINE PREVAILING PARTY AND TO FIX ATTORNEYS FEES AS COSTS (TRANSACTION ID # 100043301) FILED BY PLAINTIFF CHARTER ASSET MANAGMENT FUND, L.P.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS LATIN GRAMMAR SCHOOL, INC. AND LATIN COLLEGE PREPARATORY CHARTER SCHOOL, INC.'S MOTION TO DETERMINE PREVAILING PARTY AND FIX ATTORNEYS FEES AS COSTS. THE COURT RE-AFFIRMS THE TENTATIVE HEARING ISSUED PRIOR TO THE AUGUST 7 HEARING: DEFENDANTS LATIN GRAMMAR SCHOOL, INC. AND LATIN COLLEGE PREPARATORY CHARTER SCHOOL, INC.'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES IS DENIED. DEFENDANTS' SUCCESSFUL MOTION TO QUASH WAS NOT A DETERMINATION ON THE MERITS OF THE CASE, BUT SOLELY A RULING ON WHETHER THEY ARE SUBJECT TO BEING SUED IN CALIFORNIA ON THE CLAIMS OF PLAINTIFF CHARTER ASSET MANAGEMENT FUND, L.P. THE FINDINGS MADE ON THE MOTION TO QUASH WERE MADE FOR THE SOLE PURPOSE OF DETERMINING WHETHER THIS COURT COULD EXERCISE SPECIFIC PERSONAL JURISDICTION OVER DEFENDANTS AND WERE NOT INTENDED TO AND PRESUMABLY ARE NOT BINDING ON ANY MERITS ISSUE, ALTHOUGH THAT ISSUE MAY NEED TO BE LITIGATED IN GEORGIA. BECAUSE THE MERITS OF THE CONTRACT CLAIMS HAVE NOT BEEN DETERMINED AND CAM HAS STATED THAT IT INTENDS TO PURSUE THOSE CLAIMS IN GEORGIA, DEFENDANTS ARE NOT PREVAILING PARTIES FOR PURPOSES OF A FEES AWARD. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/HK

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-24-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Determine Prevailing Party And Fix Attorneys Fees As Costs
Off Calendar Aug-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Brian Blackman appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. S. Edward Slabach of Young, Minney & Corr LLP, Tel. No. 1-916-646-1400
appearing for the Defendants.


RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS LATIN GRAMMAR SCHOOL, INC. AND LATIN COLLEGE PREPARATORY CHARTER SCHOOL, INC.'s Motion To Determine Prevailing Party And Fix Attorneys Fees As Costs. The court re-affirms the tentative hearing issued prior to the August 7 hearing: Defendants Latin Grammar School, Inc. and Latin College Preparatory Charter School, Inc.'s motion for attorneys' fees is denied. Defendants' successful motion to quash was not a determination on the merits of the case, but solely a ruling on whether they are subject to being sued in California on the claims of plaintiff Charter Asset Management Fund, L.P. The findings made on the motion to quash were made for the sole purpose of determining whether this court could exercise specific personal jurisdiction over defendants and were not intended to and presumably are not binding on any merits issue, although that issue may need to be litigated in Georgia. Because the merits of the contract claims have not been determined and CAM has stated that it intends to pursue those claims in Georgia, defendants are not prevailing parties for purposes of a fees award. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-04-2018 Set For Order To Show Cause On Jul-31-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar May-08-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Brian Blackman, Esq. 415-500-7705 of Blaxter Blackman for Plaintiff Charter Asset Managment.
Christopher Adams, Esq. 404-835-9402 of Krevolin & Horst LLC for Defendant LATIN GRAMMAR SCHOOL, INC., LATIN COLLEGE PREPARATORY CHARTER SCHOOL, INC. through Court Call

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court share his views; the factoring company was negligent, the issue is not stated on the tentative ruling that the two parties are victims. Plaintiff argues factoring company, ostensible authority. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes)
The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS LATIN GRAMMAR SCHOOL, INC., LATIN COLLEGE PREPARATORY CHARTER SCHOOL, INC.'s Motion To Quash Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction.

Defendants Latin Grammar School, Inc. and Latin College Preparatory Charter School, Inc.'s motion to quash is granted as to both defendants. Plaintiff Charter Asset Management Fund, L.P. has not shown that either Christopher Clemons or Erica Anthony had actual or ostensible authority to bind LGS and LCP when they signed the factoring agreements. LGS and LCP did not retain either Mr. Clemons or Ms. Anthony with regard to the factoring agreements nor did they give them authority to enter into those agreements. Mr. Clemons admitted that he lacked authority to enter into the factoring agreements on behalf of LGS and LCP. While ostensible authority is a closer call than actual authority, ostensible authority does not apply when the party asserting ostensible authority acted negligently with regard to the persons claimed to have ostensible authority. (Kaplan v. Coldwell Banker Residential Affiliates, Inc. (1997) 59 Cal.App.4th 741, 747.) CAM's failure to determine, much less require, adherence to its own requirement that all members of the boards of LGS and LCP sign resolutions stating that the signatories were authorized to enter into the factoring agreements constitutes negligence precluding a finding of ostensible authority. County First Nat. Bank of Santa Cruz v. Coast Dairies & Land Co. (1941) 46 Cal.App.2d 355 is readily distinguishable because in that case the lender did not impose a requirement that the board of the borrower approve the loan. Absent actual or ostensible authority by Mr. Clemons and Ms. Anthony, there is no basis for this court to assert jurisdiction over LGS and LCP. Neither LGS nor LCP are subject to the specific jurisdiction because neither of them took any actions directed toward California.(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Gordon Aiavao, CSR#11216, gaiavao@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
Off Calendar Feb-21-2018 Continued To May-01-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LATIN GRAMMAR SCHOOL, INC., LATIN COLLEGE PREPARATORY CHARTER SCHOOL, INC.'s Motion To Quash Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction.

Defendants Latin Grammar School, Inc. and Latin College Preparatory Charter School, Inc.'s motion to quash is continued to May 1, 2018. Plaintiff Charter Asset Management Fund L.P. is granted immediate leave to conduct the following discovery limited to jurisdictional issues and/or ostensible agency: (1) no more than 20 special interrogatories regarding the relationship between Latin Grammar School, Inc. and Latin College Preparatory Charter School, Inc. and Erica Anthony and Christopher Clemons; and (2) one deposition of no more than 6 hours. Plaintiff must file and personally or e-mail serve its supplemental opposition papers by April 16, 2018. Defendants must file and personally or e-mail serve their supplemental reply papers by April 24, 2018. All parties shall provide courtesy copies of their supplemental filings with department 302 on the day the papers are filed.

Counsel for Charter Asset Management Fund is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)
